title: M1.x: Credit role normalization (normalized role + verbatim role_raw)
description: Extend the M1 Credit model so consumers get a normalized role from a controlled vocabulary AND the verbatim source label. vgmdb credit labels are freeform ('Original Music Composed by', 'Words', 'Music', 'Recording Studio') which are useless for a client trying to fill a 'composer' field, but a wrong mapping could surprise users — so keep both: normalized role for ingest + raw label for fidelity (exposed via a client option). Needs: (a) Credit model change (normalized role + role_raw), (b) a designed role vocabulary + mapping rules, (c) M3 parser applies the mapping. Discovered during M5 golden authoring (verbatim roles in 271/45000 etc.). Blocks finalizing M5 golden credit roles.
acceptance_criteria: Credit exposes a normalized role (controlled vocab) and the verbatim raw label; vocabulary + mapping defined; M5 goldens re-authored against it.
notes: Implementation COMPLETE + code-reviewed; branch credit-role-normalization pushed to origin (b2d14b9). PR auto-creation failed: gh authed as oliviernabuio but repo is technophile-musicfan/vgmdb-client (no access). Create PR manually: https://github.com/technophile-musicfan/vgmdb-client/pull/new/credit-role-normalization . Still blocks M5 G4 (vgmdb-v9g.5.4) until merged to main.
close_reason: Merged to local main (merge 79fc2d4): Role enum + normalize_role + Credit role/role_raw; models spec synced; archived; 142 tests green. origin/main push pending user's mechanism; origin branch credit-role-normalization also available for PR.

title: M5-G2: Dev-only capture script
description: Implement scripts/capture_fixtures.py (dev-only, not shipped, not in CI): load .env via python-dotenv (VGMDB_CF_CLEARANCE, VGMDB_USER_AGENT, optional VGMDB_BASE_URL); read targets from manifest.json; fetch via SyncTransport with throttle on; write ONLY raw HTML to albums/<id>.html or search/<slug>.html. Add --overwrite (skip existing by default); surface transport errors clearly (CloudflareChallengeError -> token-refresh msg). Covers tasks.md group 2.
acceptance_criteria: Script loads .env, reads manifest, writes raw HTML only; --overwrite skips existing by default; transport errors surfaced; excluded from shipped package + CI.
close_reason: Implemented & committed (73d9d53): dataset scaffold + manifest (candidate ids) + python-dotenv dev dep; dev-only capture script with --overwrite/--only + clear CF error handling. ruff/mypy clean.

title: M2 Transport: httpx sync+async, cf_clearance, typed errors
description: httpx-based fetcher (sync + async). Manual cf_clearance cookie + matching User-Agent injection. Retries. Typed errors distinguishing a Cloudflare challenge from a genuine 404/not-found. Runtime does not attempt to beat Cloudflare.
acceptance_criteria: Sync and async fetch of a vgmdb URL with injected cf_clearance/UA; retry on transient failure; distinct exception types for CF-challenge vs 404.
notes: MVP transport delivered and merged to main (b716114): all 6 impl tasks (4.1-4.6) closed; 71 tests, ruff/mypy/deptry green; spec archived to openspec/specs/transport. Epic kept open for deferred enhancements 4.7 (debug logging) and 4.8 (throttle concurrency/per-retry spacing).
close_reason: M2 Transport complete & merged to local main: sync+async httpx transport, cf_clearance, typed errors, retry/throttle, per-attempt+async-safe throttle, debug logging. 8/8 children done; 207 tests green.

title: M1 Models (core): Album, Track, Credit, search results
description: pydantic v2 models forming the shared vocabulary every layer speaks: Album, Track, Credit, and search-result types. Foundation for parsers, client, and fixtures golden outputs.
acceptance_criteria: Album/Track/Credit and search-result models defined as pydantic v2; importable from the package; typed and documented.
close_reason: M1 models delivered and merged to main (7b88cf8): LocalizedText/PartialDate/ArtistRef value types, Album/Disc/Track/Credit entities, albums-only search; frozen+extra-forbid pydantic v2; 37 model tests (108 total) green; spec archived to openspec/specs/models.

title: MVP: first usable vgmdb-client (album + search)
description: First usable version of vgmdb-client: a typed, direct-to-vgmdb.net Python library returning pydantic models. Covers Albums-by-ID and Search, sync + async, over a shared parsing/model core, with a caller-supplied cf_clearance token. See docs/superpowers/2026-06-14_vgmdb_client.md and _feature_map.md.
design: Clean-room HTML parsing (no hufman in runtime path). Layers: Models (pydantic v2), Transport (httpx sync+async, manual cf_clearance/UA, typed CF-challenge vs 404 errors), Parsers (pure html->model), Client API (Client/AsyncClient). Fixture-based tests.
close_reason: MVP COMPLETE & merged to local main: M1 models, M2 transport (sync+async, cf_clearance, retry/throttle/logging), M3 clean-room parsers, M4 Client/AsyncClient public API, M5 fixtures, M1.x credit role normalization. End-to-end album+search pipeline works; 207 tests green, ruff+mypy clean. 6/6 children done.

title: B1 Deep-parse helper (LLM/ML) for freeform fields
description: Opt-in enrichment of freeform fields (track-level contributions, detailed roles from the Notes/Information tab). Pluggable backend: first an optional LLM_URL -> OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint; embedded lightweight ML later. Shipped as an opt-in extra (e.g. vgmdb-client[llm]).
acceptance_criteria: Given parsed album + raw freeform text, helper returns enriched structured fields via an OpenAI-compatible endpoint configured by LLM_URL; degrades gracefully when unset; backend is pluggable.
notes: M5 fixtures confirm per-track credits live in freeform notes (e.g. album 271 tracks 10/11/27; album 33000 'Syrufit (2,3,5,7,10 -- Compose:1,9)'). These goldens' notes fields are ready-made inputs/test cases for the B1 deep-parse.
close_reason: B1 deep-parse delivered + merged to main (141dad1): enrich package (AlbumEnrichment overlay, EnrichmentBackend protocol, OpenAICompatibleBackend + backend_from_env, enrich_album graceful-empty), opsx change archived + enrich spec synced, code-review done, 217 tests green. Sub-tasks .1.1-.1.4 closed.

title: Beta: measure & enrich (deep-parse + harness + more entities)
description: Adds opt-in LLM/ML deep-parse of freeform fields, a parsing-quality comparison harness (hufman vs ours vs ours+LLM), and more entities (Artist/Product/Organization). See docs/superpowers/2026-06-14_vgmdb_client_feature_map.md.
design: Deep-parse backend: optional LLM_URL -> OpenAI-compatible endpoint first; embedded lightweight ML later. hufman (MIT) vendored as dev-only benchmark dependency, never in runtime.
close_reason: Beta epic complete: B1 deep-parse (enrich), B2 quality harness, B3 more entities (Artist/Product/Organization) all delivered + merged to main. 265 tests green; ruff+mypy clean.

title: B3 follow-up: broaden entity fixture coverage
description: Each B3 entity has a single fixture (artist 146 Yasunori Mitsuda = person; product 262 Streets of Rage; org 17 Victor Musical Industries). Add fixtures that exercise more parser paths: a unit-type Artist (to cover the members field, currently always [] in the golden), and a Japanese-named Organization (org 17 name is English-only, so the has_cjk Japanese-key branch in parse_organization._name is untested). Capture needs live cf_clearance. Discovered during B3 (vgmdb-bzf.3, merged 3de6952).

title: B3 follow-up: populate Artist/Product type fields
description: parse_artist and parse_product currently leave Artist.type / Product.type = None: those vgmdb pages have no cleanly-labeled type field (unlike Organization, which exposes a Type dt/dd -> 'Label / Imprint'). Investigate reliable verbatim sources: Product category appears as a 'Multimedia Franchise' span in a div.smallfont near the title; Artist person-vs-unit is implicit (birthdate=>person, members=>unit) with no explicit label. Decide whether to extract these verbatim or add a normalized enum. Keep selectors clean-room and golden-stable. Discovered during B3 (vgmdb-bzf.3, merged 3de6952).

title: parsers: align multi-language tracks by track number, not positional index
description: parse_album._discs merges per-language tracklist tabs by positional disc/track index, assuming every language tab mirrors the disc/track structure. All M5 fixtures satisfy this, but a page whose non-primary tab groups discs/tracks differently would mis-attach or drop a localized title. Match by (disc number, track number) instead of index. Surfaced in M3 code-review; no current fixture triggers it.

title: Transport: throttle concurrency + per-retry spacing (code-review follow-up)
description: Code-review findings on the simple politeness throttle: (1) AsyncTransport._throttle reads/writes self._last without synchronization, so concurrent get() coroutines race and defeat min_interval under concurrency; (2) the throttle is applied once per get() before the retry loop, so retried attempts within one get() are spaced only by backoff, not min_interval. Consider an async-safe gate (lock/semaphore) and applying the throttle per HTTP attempt.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrent async get() calls honor min_interval; retried attempts are throttled; covered by tests.
close_reason: Implemented + tested: per-attempt throttle, async lock-gated throttle, debug logging. 207 tests green.

title: Transport: debug-level per-request logging (D7 deferral)
description: design.md D7 specifies stdlib logging with debug-level per-request logs (method, path, status, attempt) in SyncTransport/AsyncTransport. Deferred from the transport-layer change: it is not a spec requirement and was not in tasks.md. Add a module logger emitting debug logs per request/attempt.
acceptance_criteria: Both clients emit debug-level logs per request (method, path, status, attempt) via logging.getLogger(__name__); covered by a test (e.g. caplog).
close_reason: Implemented + tested: per-attempt throttle, async lock-gated throttle, debug logging. 207 tests green.

title: V1c Release polish: docs site + PyPI publish
description: Full MkDocs documentation site with examples, and PyPI publishing of vgmdb-client. Final polish gate for V1.
acceptance_criteria: Docs site builds and deploys; package published to PyPI; install + quickstart verified from a clean environment.

title: V1b Remaining entities: Events, releases, etc.
description: Add models + clean-room parsers + client methods for Events, releases, and any remaining vgmdb entity types not covered in MVP/Beta.
acceptance_criteria: Remaining entity fetchers (sync+async) return validated models, fixture-tested.

title: V1a Auth-token helper: fill/renew cf_clearance
description: Assist the user in filling/renewing the cf_clearance token (UX/design TBD). Explicitly NOT about defeating Cloudflare login/challenge. Builds on Transport.
acceptance_criteria: A documented helper flow that obtains/refreshes a working cf_clearance + matching UA and feeds it to the client; no automated CF-challenge defeat.

title: V1: complete & polished (auth helper + remaining entities + release)
description: Completes the library: cf_clearance auth-token fill/renew helper, remaining entities (Events, releases), full docs site and PyPI publish. See docs/superpowers/2026-06-14_vgmdb_client_feature_map.md.
design: Auth helper assists token fill/renew, does NOT defeat Cloudflare. Release polish: MkDocs site + PyPI.
